title: salix atrocinerea subsp. jahandiezii
intro: Salix atrocinerea subsp. jahandiezii, also known as the Atlas willow, is a subspecies of the black willow (Salix atrocinerea), a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Atlas Mountains in North Africa. The Atlas willow has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Salix atrocinerea subsp. jahandiezii include its ability to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the Atlas willow can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
